kernel_of_truth.finding: 7-day daily-regen HOLD streak (2026-04-22 to 2026-04-28) is the substantive observation. Daily AI-regen pipeline IS running on schedule (timestamps and tracking.html rows update), but registry counts (confirmed=69 / refined=4 / prospective=10) are byte-identical across 7 consecutive regens. This is NOT bug — the regen mechanism works; what is held is the WIN registry itself.
charitable_alternatives_considered: Author intentionally deferring registry updates while preparing larger batch (legitimate but should produce SOME signal in commits or page text — there are none).
Daily-regen script buggy/looping without output (rejected — it does update daily_review.json timestamps and reverts speculative DW row insertions, so it is functioning).

Pipeline is genuinely steady-state because no new test results have arrived warranting reclassification (plausible — but the registry is supposed to be the mechanism for catching test outcomes, and 7 consecutive HOLDs across the same set with PRED-105 visibly past close is anomalous).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_supports_existing_4_1_framing: EXP-270 (integrated 2026-04-28) framed the sigma growth as Fisher sqrt(n) mechanical within 1.4% of predicted value (47.69 → 61.46 = 1.378x ≈ sqrt(887/520) = 1.306x; predicted 62.30 vs observed 61.46). The 7-day HOLD corroborates: the mechanism producing sigma growth is sample-size accumulation, not new evidence-based reclassification. If new physics or signal were entering the pipeline, the registry counts would shift. They do not.

The Fisher sqrt(n) vindication framing in EXP-270 was integrated 2026-04-28T13:28Z; this analysis run is the first to reference it as already-published. If curmudgeon flags overreach in 4.1, that goes through the standard EXP/issue path — not this normal-mode record.
counter_argument_per_operator_pref: Could the 7-day HOLD reflect something other than mechanical counting? The dome could be in a legitimate quiet period awaiting external test data. But (1) the registry is the mechanism for absorbing test outcomes, (2) 11 prospective_pending entries in status_history.json suggest there ARE candidate test results queued — they are not being absorbed, (3) PRED-105 has visibly closed and posted no outcome. Mechanical counting remains the most parsimonious explanation; a legitimate-quiet alternative would predict zero queue growth, not 11 prospective_pending entries sitting un-acted-upon.
